prose: Frontier orbital energies for aniline in the gas phase were computed using density-functional theory (DFT), specifically B3LYP/def2-TZVP, with density fitting enabled, under the tier 'standard'. The electronic SCF cycle converged successfully in 9 iterations. The HOMO–LUMO gap was computed as 5.3048177 eV. HOMO energy: -5.553772 eV; LUMO energy: -0.2489544 eV. This calculation used PySCF and ran on the input file aniline.xyz located at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz. The final electronic energy was -7829.1453 eV (also recorded in Hartree: -287.71578). No warnings were reported during the computation. All integrity checks (finite energy, SCF convergence, correct schema report) passed.
